meanings.definition: grant freedom to; free from confinement
turn loose or free from restraint

examples: loosen the tension on a rope

meanings.definition: become loose or looser or less tight
not compact or dense in structure or arrangement
(of a ball in sport) not in the possession or control of any player
not tight; not closely constrained or constricted or constricting
examples: the large shoes were very loose
a loose organization of the local farmers
a loose interpretation of what she had been told
a free translation of the poem
the old man's skin hung loose and grey

meanings.definition: (of textures) full of small openings or gaps
lacking a sense of restraint or responsibility
not carefully arranged in a package

examples: criminals on the loose in the neighborhood

meanings.definition: casual and unrestrained in sexual behavior
examples: he was told to avoid loose (or light) women
cows in India are running loose
